purpose: Keep Hermes self-improvement, project-drift detection, and repo operations as separate lanes with separate evidence and gates.

lanes.name: Hermes self-improvement
lanes.purpose: Improve the local agent harness, prompts, tests, and reusable workflows without changing goto product behavior unless explicitly selected as a repo task.
manual_gates: Changing global Hermes config, credentials, providers, delivery targets, or scheduler jobs
Turning an advisory harness into an autonomous executor
Adding broad abstractions without a failing test or repeated use case

lanes.name: Project drift and divergence
lanes.purpose: Detect and reconcile mismatch between docs, plans, harness metadata, generated state, CI, and live repository facts.
lanes.primary_output: drift finding, targeted doc/metadata repair, or explicit waived mismatch
manual_gates: Changing product goals or architecture decisions
Deleting historical evidence instead of marking it stale
Rewriting release or user-facing claims without verification

lanes.name: Project operations
lanes.purpose: Run routine repo operations such as verification, branch/PR loops, release readiness checks, and safe maintenance with evidence and rollback boundaries.
lanes.primary_output: verified branch/PR/check result, operation log, or safe stop report
manual_gates: Release, deploy, main push, or public announcement
User/system side effects such as /Applications installs, shell rc edits, Finder restarts, sudo, service managers
